title: गोपिका गीतम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गोपिका गीतम् ॥

जयति तेऽधिकं जन्मना व्रजः
श्रयत इन्दिरा शश्वदत्र हि।
दयित दृश्यतां दिक्षु तावका-
स्त्वयि धृतासवस्त्वां विचिन्वते॥ १॥

शरदुदाशये साधुजातसत्-
सरसिजोदरश्रीमुषा दृशा।
सुरतनाथ तेऽशुल्कदासिका
वरद निघ्नतो नेह किं वधः॥ २॥

विषजलाप्ययाद् व्यालराक्षसाद्
वर्षमारुताद् वैद्युतानलात्।
वृषमयात्मजाद् विश्वतोभयाद्
ऋषभ ते वयं रक्षिता मुहुः॥ ३॥

न खलु गोपिकानन्दनो भवान्
अखिलदेहिनामन्तरात्मदृक्।
विखनसार्थितो विश्वगुप्तये
सख उदेयिवान् सात्वतां कुले॥ ४॥

विरचिताभयं वृष्णिधुर्य ते
चरणमीयुषां संसृतेर्भयात्।
करसरोरुहं कान्त कामदं
शिरसि धेहि नः श्रीकरग्रहम्॥ ५॥

व्रजजनार्तिहन् वीर योषितां
निजजनस्मयध्वंसनस्मित।
भज सखे भवत्किंकरीः स्म नो
जलरुहाननं चारु दर्शय॥ ६॥

प्रणतदेहिनां पापकर्शनं
तृणचरानुगं श्रीनिकेतनम्।
फणिफणार्पितं ते पदाम्बुजं
कृणु कुचेषु नः कृन्धि हृच्छयम्॥ ७॥

मधुरया गिरा वल्गुवाक्यया
बुधमनोज्ञया पुष्करेक्षण।
विधिकरीरिमा वीर मुह्यती-
रधरसीधुनाऽऽप्याययस्व नः॥ ८॥

तव कथामृतं तप्तजीवनं
कविभिरीडितं कल्मषापहम्।
श्रवणमंगलं श्रीमदाततं
भुवि गृणन्ति ये भूरिदा जनाः॥ ९॥

इति गोपिकागीतं सम्पूर्णम्।
श्रीमद्भागवतमहापुराणे दशमस्कन्धे एकत्रिंशोऽध्यायः॥

॥ इति श्री गोपिका गीतम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gopika Gitam ||

Jayati te'dhikam janmana Vrajah
Shrayata Indira shashvad-atra hi |
Dayita drishyatam dikshu tavakah
Tvayi dhritasavas-tvam chicinvate || 1 ||

Sharad-udashaye sadhu-jatasat-
Sarasijodara-shri-musha drisha |
Surata-natha te'shulka-dasika
Varada nighnato neha kim vadhah || 2 ||

Visha-jalapyayad vyala-rakshasad
Varsha-marutad vaidyutanalat |
Vrishamayatmajad vishvato-bhayad
Rishabha te vayam rakshita muhuh || 3 ||

Na khalu Gopika-nandano bhavan
Akhila-dehinam-antaratma-drik |
Vikhanasarthito vishva-guptaye
Sakha udeyivan Satvatam kule || 4 ||

Virachitabhayam Vrishni-dhurya te
Charanam-iyusham samsriter-bhayat |
Kara-saroruham kanta kamadam
Shirasi dhehi nah Shri-kara-graham || 5 ||

Vraja-janaarti-han vira yoshitam
Nija-jana-smaya-dhvamsana-smita |
Bhaja sakhe bhavat-kinkarin sma no
Jala-ruhananam charu darshaya || 6 ||

Pranata-dehinam papa-karshanam
Trina-charanu-gam Shri-niketanam |
Phani-phanarpitam te padambujam
Krinu kucheshu nah krindhi hrichchhayam || 7 ||

Madhuraya gira valgu-vakyaya
Budha-manojnaya Pushkarekshana |
Vidhi-karir-ima vira muhyatir
Adhara-sidhuna''pyayayasva nah || 8 ||

Tava kathamritam tapta-jivanam
Kavibhir-iditam kalmashapaham |
Shravana-mangalam shrimad-atatam
Bhuvi grinanti ye bhurida janah || 9 ||

Iti Gopika-gitam sampurnam |
Shrimad-Bhagavata-Mahapurane Dashama-skandhe Ekatrimsho'dhyayah ||

|| Iti Shri Gopika Gitam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे प्रिय, तुम्हारे जन्म से व्रज की महान जय हो। यहाँ लक्ष्मी सदा निवास करती हैं। प्रिय, सभी दिशाओं में तुम्हारी प्रिय गोपियाँ, जिन्होंने तुम्हारे लिए प्राण धारण किए हैं, तुम्हें खोज रही हैं॥ १॥

हे शरद ऋतु के निर्मल आकाश में उत्पन्न कमल की शोभा हरने वाली दृष्टि वाले, हे सुरतनाथ, हम तुम्हारी अमूल्य दासियाँ हैं। हे वरद, हमें मारना क्या वध नहीं है?॥ २॥

विषमय जल से, सर्प रूप राक्षस से, वर्षा की आँधी से, बिजली की आग से, वृषभासुर से, सर्वत्र भय से - हे श्रेष्ठ, तुमने हमारी बार-बार रक्षा की॥ ३॥

तुम गोपिकानन्दन (गोपी के पुत्र) नहीं हो, तुम समस्त देहधारियों के अन्तरात्मा के द्रष्टा हो। ब्रह्मा द्वारा प्रार्थित, विश्व की रक्षा के लिए, हे सखे, तुम सात्वत (यादव) कुल में प्रकट हुए हो॥ ४॥

हे वृष्णि श्रेष्ठ, हे कान्त, संसार के भय से तुम्हारे चरणों की शरण में आई हुई हमारे मस्तक पर अपना कामद, अभयदायक, श्री का ग्रहण करने वाला करकमल रखो॥ ५॥

हे व्रजजनों का दुःख हरने वाले वीर, हे अपने जनों के अभिमान का नाश करने वाली मुस्कान वाले, हे सखे, अपनी दासियों को स्वीकार करो, अपना सुन्दर कमलमुख दिखाओ॥ ६॥

प्रणत जीवों के पाप का नाश करने वाले, तृणचर (गायों) के अनुगामी, लक्ष्मी के निकेतन, सर्पफण पर स्थापित तुम्हारे चरण कमल हमारे वक्ष पर रखो, हमारे हृदय की व्यथा दूर करो॥ ७॥

मधुर वाणी से, सुन्दर वचनों से, विद्वानों को मनोज्ञ लगने वाली, हे कमलनयन, हे वीर, विधि की दासी ये मोहित हो रही हैं, अधरामृत से हमें जीवित करो॥ ८॥

तुम्हारी कथामृत तप्त जीवों का जीवन है, कवियों द्वारा गाया गया, कलुष को दूर करने वाला, श्रवण मंगल, श्रीसम्पन्न और व्यापक है। जो इसे पृथ्वी पर गाते हैं, वे सबसे बड़े दानी हैं॥ ९॥
benefits: Sacred song of the Gopis from Srimad Bhagavatam (10.31)
Contains the famous verse 'Tava kathamritam' on the glory of Hari Katha
Awakens divine love (prema) in the heart of the devotee
Deepens understanding of the Gopis' supreme devotion
Purifies the heart and removes all sins
Invokes the blessing of the highest spiritual emotion of divine love
description: Gopika Gitam — गोपिका गीतम् — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Krishna. This sacred composition is sourced from Srimad Bhagavatam (10.31) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to contain the famous verse 'Tava kathamritam' on the glory of Hari Katha, purify the heart and remove all sins, and awaken divine love (prema) in the heart of the devotee. This sacred text is ideally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami and Sharad Purnima for enhanced blessings. With 9 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Gopika Gitam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gopika Gitam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopika Gitam (गोपिका गीतम् — literally Song of the Gopikas) is the celebrated 19-verse Sanskrit lament sung by the gopis (cowherd maidens, also called gopikas) of Vrindavana when Sri Krishna suddenly disappeared from the Maha-Rasa dance on the autumn full moon night of Sharad Purnima. The hymn is preserved as Chapter 31 (Ekatrimsho'dhyayah) of the Tenth Canto (Dashama-skandha) of the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana. Gopika Gitam is the alternate Sanskrit-traditional name for the same composition more popularly known as Gopi Geet — both names refer to the identical text from Bhagavatam 10.31, with Gopika being the formal Sanskrit spelling and Gopi the colloquial Hindi-Brajbhasha rendering. The 19 verses combine viraha-bhakti (separation-love), philosophical insight (verse 4 declaring Krishna the antaratma-drik of all beings), surrender (verse 5 begging for the lotus hand on their heads), and the supreme verse on Hari-katha (verse 9 — Tava kathamritam tapta-jivanam). (Source: Srimad Bhagavatam 10.31.)

seoFaqs.question: Where in the Bhagavata Mahapurana does the Gopika Gitam occur?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopika Gitam constitutes the entire Chapter 31 of the Tenth Canto (Dashama-skandha) of the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana, the supreme scripture of Krishna-bhakti compiled by Veda-Vyasa. Specifically it is part of the Rasa-pancha-adhyayi — the five chapters (29-33) at the very heart of the Bhagavata that narrate the Maha-Rasa lila on Sharad Purnima night: chapter 29 (Rasa-arambha — Krishna calls the gopis with his flute), chapter 30 (Gopi-anveshana — gopis search for Krishna in the forest after he disappears), chapter 31 (Gopika Gitam — the lament-prayer itself), chapter 32 (Krishna's reappearance and dialogue), chapter 33 (the Maha-Rasa dance where Krishna multiplies to dance with each gopi). The Bhagavata's authentic colophon at the end of the Gopika Gitam reads: Iti Gopika-gitam sampurnam, Shrimad-Bhagavata-Mahapurane Dashama-skandhe Ekatrimsho'dhyayah. (Source: Srimad Bhagavatam Skandha 10, chapter 31.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Jayati Te Adhikam Janmana Vrajah — the opening verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse — Jayati te-adhikam janmana Vrajah, shrayata Indira shashvad-atra hi, dayita drishyatam dikshu tavakas, tvayi dhritasavah-tvam vichinvate (Bhagavata 10.31.1) — is one of the most theologically loaded openings in all Sanskrit literature. Word-by-word: jayati te-adhikam janmana Vrajah — Vraja (Vrindavana) has been raised to supreme glory by your birth; shrayata Indira shashvat atra hi — Lakshmi herself perpetually takes refuge here (in Vraja, considering it superior to even Vaikuntha); dayita drishyatam dikshu — O beloved, please be visible in the directions; tavakah — your own (gopis), tvayi dhritasavah — holding their life-breath only in you, tvam vichinvate — are searching for you. The verse establishes Vraja-bhumi (the soil of Vrindavana) as superior to even Vaikuntha because Krishna chose to be born there, and Lakshmi herself perpetually performs tapasya in Vraja for participation in the Maha-Rasa. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.31.1.)

seoFaqs.question: Are the Gopika Gitam and the Gopi Geet the same composition?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — Gopika Gitam (गोपिका गीतम्) and Gopi Geet (गोपी गीत) refer to the identical 19-verse composition from Chapter 31 of the Tenth Canto of the Srimad Bhagavata Mahapurana. The two names differ only in linguistic register: Gopika Gitam is the formal Sanskrit-traditional designation used in classical commentaries (Sridhara Svami, Vishvanatha Chakravarti, Madhusudana Saraswati, Vallabha Acharya, Madhva Acharya, Sukadeva-pratyaksha-bhashya, Shri Bhashya tradition) and in the Bhagavata's own colophon — gopika being the Sanskrit feminine for cowherd-maiden; Gopi Geet is the Hindi-Brajbhasha-Bengali colloquial form used in modern bhajana, satsanga and devotional discourse — gopi being the abbreviated everyday form. The text, content, sequence and theological significance are identical. The Sthotra-pages on this site treat them as twin entries serving the two search-spellings devotees use. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 10.31; Vaishnava commentary tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Gopika Gitam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopika Gitam is most powerful on Sharad Purnima (Ashvin Shukla Purnima — the autumn full moon night when the Maha-Rasa actually occurred; the supreme festival of the Maha-rasa at Vrindavana), Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — Krishna's birth), Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's birthday), Govardhana Puja (Kartik Shukla Pratipada), Gopashtami, Kartika Vrata month, all twenty-four Ekadashis (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Mokshada Ekadashi), and on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar). Daily recitation in the morning or evening sandhya, especially during Chaturmasya months, is the standard practice of Gaudiya, Pushtimargi, Nimbarka, Vallabha and Madhva Vaishnavas. The Gopika Gitam is also a central reading-text during Bhagavata Saptaha (the seven-day complete recitation of the Bhagavata) — typically read on day five when the Krishna-charita of Skandha 10 is reached. (Sources: Vaishnava sampradaya tradition; Bhagavata Saptaha vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does reciting the Gopika Gitam grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Gopika Gitam: (1) awakening of madhura-viraha-prema — the supreme bhakti-rasa of separation, considered the highest form of love because in viraha the beloved is more constantly present in remembrance than in actual union, (2) the kalmasha-apaham promise of verse 9 — destruction of all sins through Hari-katha-shravana, since the very utterance of Krishna-katha is purifying, (3) realisation of Krishna as antaratma-drik — the inner-witness Self of all beings (verse 4), beyond mere external Vrindavana-leela, (4) the merit of joining the gopikas themselves in their supreme love — sahacharya-bhakti with Krishna's most intimate companions, (5) blessings of Lakshmi herself who is described as perpetually taking refuge in Vraja (verse 1), (6) admission into the Goloka Vrindavana eternal Maha-Rasa through bhava-tadatmya, and (7) liberation through suddha-bhakti as supremely taught by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. (Sources: Bhagavata phala-shruti; Vaishnava acharya commentaries.)
